opp.ais_why:
• Chris Kelliher engaged the April 1 2026 demo on AI scoring and autonomous sourcing but framed his questions around trust and engine logic rather than commitment. The AutoClass AI engine and conversational analytics for natural-language supplier queries received attention, but the buyer's posture stayed in capability probe mode rather than champion advocacy. AI importance is rated Important.
• Merlin, Merlin Intake, Agentic AI and ANA are in the product scope but the buyer's RFP scope was confirmed at supplier onboarding, sourcing and contract management - source-to-contract modules with GEP as the named competitor. Generative AI for RFP question creation via Rfxquest G and the Risk Radar monitoring 40 risk types were demonstrated heavily by Zycus, with the buyer's feedback staying cautious.
• Verbatim previously captured at action-item level; full transcript reviewed via keyword filter. Two completed calls show Chris Kelliher and Leon Pilosof present consistently, with the action item limited to providing additional collateral and RFP-drafting support. With no explicit AI champion and the conversation focused on GEP-replacement S2C functionality, this remains Curious.

champion.narrative: No champion exists. Leon Pilosof and Chris Kelliher are neutral RFP evaluators executing a multi-vendor benchmark; neither has advocated for Zycus, made introductions to power, or pushed back on competitors. Leon stated 6 March 2026 'we did consider Zycus the last time we went to market' (past consideration, not current preference) and positioned Levelpath as the aspirational standard ('what a future S2C should look like'). No contact has shared internal decision criteria, warned of competitive moves, or driven the Zycus agenda. Victoria Metoyer Reese is an IT Coordinator who schedules meetings but has no procurement or budget authority. Christopher Furst (CIO) is an account-level contact but has never engaged on this opportunity. Without a champion to influence demo scoring, down-select decisions, and economic buyer access, Zycus is at risk of being out-positioned by Levelpath's modern narrative or GEP's incumbent relationship. The move is to convert Leon or Chris into an active coach by demonstrating differentiated value during the July demos and securing their advocacy for the down-select.

competition.narrative: Competition is confirmed, multi-vendor, and structured around a formal RFP benchmark. Four competitors are active: (1) GEP is the incumbent (6-year relationship, GEP Smart was stable, Quantum upgrade failed) and is re-engaging (Leon received GEP onboarding invite 27 April 2026 per Next_Step; Leon stated 6 March 'we are giving them an opportunity, they have a list of things they need to fix'). If GEP resolves the Quantum pain points, relationship inertia and renewal pricing give them advantage. Threat level: high, status: incumbent re-engaging. (2) Levelpath is the aspirational competitor; Leon stated 6 March after seeing their demo the prior week 'that's what a future S2C should look like in terms of integrated AI, good AI and agents and user interface.' High buyer enthusiasm for Levelpath's modern positioning. Threat level: high, status: active preferred-fit peer. (3) Coupa and (4) SAP Ariba are named by Leon 6 March as part of the RFP benchmark ('we're going to be looking at Coupa and Ariba as well as other vendors') but no buyer sentiment or differentiation discussion. Threat level: medium, status: active RFP participants. Zycus wins by proving enterprise reliability AND modern AI-forward UX during scripted July demos, quantifying ROI to justify replacement cost, and converting Leon or Chris into a champion who can influence scoring and down-select.

identify_pain.narrative: Pain is operationally confirmed and owned by Chris Kelliher (Director of Procurement reporting to Leon). Chris articulated 6 March 2026 that the GEP Quantum upgrade broke a previously stable GEP Smart platform, causing: lost contract notifications, documents disappearing and converting to TXT files, contract creation complexity requiring manual rework, metadata extraction accuracy below 50 percent (payment terms, currencies, dates consistently wrong), no contract summarization beyond a few inaccurate metadata fields, supplier access issues (one in ten suppliers cannot log in), and zero AI-driven sourcing efficiency gains (no vendor event reminders, manual Q&A, poor formatting, collaboration failures). Chris stated 'any efficiencies gained through AI have been completely lost through duplicate work and troubleshooting.' The pain owner is the two-person procurement team (Leon and Chris) who absorb the manual rework. Pain is confirmed operational and acute; it is not yet quantified financially (no cost of rework, no cycle-time delta, no headcount impact) which limits the business case for replacement versus GEP fixing the issues.

decision_criteria.narrative: Decision criteria are explicitly stated and structured around RFP functional and technical compliance. Leon outlined 6 March 2026 the evaluation will assess: functional/technical compliance matrix with met/future/custom/configurable ratings, agent inventory and licensing model, SLAs, SOC and security certifications, product roadmap, and scripted live demos scored against use-case requirements. Lionsgate is benchmarking all major S2C suites (Zycus, GEP, Levelpath, Coupa, Ariba) and will down-select to finalists for deep-dive scripted demonstrations. Specific pain-driven criteria include: contract metadata extraction accuracy, notification reliability, ease of contract creation, AI-driven sourcing automation (vendor reminders, Q&A, formatting, collaboration), intake integration with the relevant stakeholder, and spend analytics dashboards. Leon emphasized 6 March modern UI and integrated AI as table stakes, stating Levelpath 'is what a future S2C should look like.' Criteria are confirmed from the buyer's words; Zycus must prove enterprise reliability AND modern AI-forward UX to compete with Levelpath's positioning.

north_star_verdict.headline: Deal is live and engaged with clear buyer-driven timeline and structured RFP process, but demonstrations scheduled to begin after 4 July 2026 and contract negotiation planned for August 2026 cannot realistically close by 7 March 2027 without continued momentum. GEP incumbent pressure and Levelpath's strong positioning create competitive headwinds. flags: Champion gap in a multi-vendor RFP: Leon (DM) and Chris are engaged but neutral; no evidence of advocacy, internal selling, or competitive deflection. In a fair, methodical RFP against GEP (incumbent), Levelpath (high buyer interest), Coupa, and Ariba, a champion is critical to influence the down-select and scripted demo scoring. Action: convert Leon or Chris into an active advocate by demonstrating measurable superiority on contract metadata accuracy (>90% vs GEP <50%) and sourcing automation during July demos, and by offering differentiated value (Merlin Intake in Teams, ANA agents) that solves their operational pain.

No quantified value case: Chris articulated significant operational pain (lost productivity, manual rework, <50% metadata accuracy, no sourcing efficiency) but no financial impact (dollar savings, headcount cost, cycle-time reduction) has been stated or built. Leon will need an ROI story to justify the replacement and secure budget approval. Action: before the July demos, co-create a value model with Leon/Chris quantifying time saved (contract metadata accuracy improvement, sourcing automation), error reduction, and potential headcount leverage for the two-person team. competitive_position.summary: Zycus is one of four to five vendors in a competitive RFP benchmark; no preferential positioning. GEP (incumbent, 6-year relationship) is the primary current threat: if they fix Quantum pain points, renewal inertia and pricing favor them. Levelpath is the strongest peer threat: Leon called them 'what a future S2C should look like' after their demo, creating high buyer enthusiasm for modern AI-forward narrative. Coupa and Ariba are active RFP participants but no buyer sentiment expressed. Zycus must prove BOTH enterprise reliability (contract, sourcing, spend analytics depth) AND modern AI-forward UX (Merlin intake, autonomous sourcing/negotiation agents) during July scripted demos to compete with Levelpath's aspirational positioning and counter GEP's relationship leverage. Without a champion and without a quantified ROI case, Zycus is at risk of being out-positioned on either modernity (vs Levelpath) or cost (vs GEP renewal).
